title: Two Guys watch Evangelion for the first time DEAD
display_title: Two Guys watch Evangelion for the first time DEAD
wikitext:
[]: Two Guys watch Evangelion for the first time... (DEAD) Post by FrDougal9000 » Sun May 12, 2013 10:40 am Quite recently, I've been talking about Evangelion to my friends at school and after much nagging, I finally got them to watch one of the episodes, which they decided to do a commentary on. Which episode was it, may you ask? The English Dub of Episode 9: Both of You, Dance Like You Want To Win! Behold the results and enjoy the madness: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=53Tc8wTBmqw (Part 1) http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mMojYZ4rzlk (Part 2) EDIT: For the sake of clarification, this is not supposed to be taken seriously...at all! This is basically what happens when you show the silliest episode of the series to a couple of guys out of context who were just simply having fun with it. I talked to the guys recently about this forum and magicalgiraffe (the one with the deeper voice) apologizes if he ended up offending anyone by accident. Don't take this riffing commentary seriously and just have fun with it, 'kay? SECOND EDIT: The videos have recently been taken down; not because of copyright but because the guys who did the commentary requested me to. They watched it recently and they didn't think it was very good so they asked me to take it down. As such, the links are now broken and the video's can't be seen any more. I apologize for the inconvenience.

[]: Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun May 12, 2013 12:14 pm That was more hilarious than I expected it to be.

[]: Post by robersora » Sun May 12, 2013 12:27 pm Well, that's why I hesitate to show anybody Evangelion... I'm too much in love with this series to let anybody shit over it with their words. (I know, this is not healthy, it's a show) Yet, it's interesting to see which little details different people notice.

[]: Post by MoroNoKimi » Sun May 12, 2013 1:30 pm I must be kind of humorless...agreeing with the above guy, it just makes me sad to see it being, well...not truly appreciated, for a series I love so very much. Mocked by people who don't understand it. Everyone notices different things, I guess. Doesn't help that I think the dub is a goddamn crime against the whole series.

[]: Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun May 12, 2013 1:32 pm I don't judge against people that make fun of the series. Just remember that they mock it because usually, they haven't put time into trying to understand the series. Now when I mock it, it's another story entirely. Nyak Nyak.

[]: Post by Justacrazyguy » Sun May 12, 2013 1:39 pm Is it me or do some fans like that there are people that mock/don´t like it just so they can feel superior?

[]: Post by C.A.P. » Sun May 12, 2013 1:44 pm This is making me wish Beavis and Butthead watched anime.

[]: Post by MoroNoKimi » Sun May 12, 2013 1:44 pm I've certainly seen such behavior in the past, usually from general anime fans who've decided that it's cool to dislike Eva because it's revolutionary/popular and thus deem it "overrated."

[]: Post by Rei IV » Sun May 12, 2013 1:52 pm MoroNoKimi wrote: I've certainly seen such behavior in the past, usually from general anime fans who've decided that it's cool to dislike Eva because it's revolutionary/popular and thus deem it "overrated."

[]: Post by NemZ » Sun May 12, 2013 2:05 pm pointless.

[]: Post by Jayfive » Sun May 12, 2013 5:02 pm Most of the...huh...humour seems to be derived from the fact they dont know what the hell is going on. Wasnt exactly a laugh riot but amusing in places. it just makes me sad to see it being, well...not truly appreciated

[]: Post by LeoXiao » Sun May 12, 2013 9:37 pm get them to watch moar. also, some people itt are taking this rather...seriously.

[]: Post by pwhodges » Mon May 13, 2013 6:34 am That was a waste of time... They don't even have a chance of being properly funny without knowing a bit more about it (and to be fair, going straight in at that episode with no background would be pretty mystifying), so most of it is just stupid-stupid, not even funny-stupid.

[]: Post by Shinoyami65 » Mon May 13, 2013 7:13 am To be fair, they have no idea what's going on, they're watching the least serious episode of Evangelion, they're watching the terrible English dub, and they're using the rather crude original subtitles. I think anyone would react pretty much the same way, especially if they were trying to make light of what they see like these guys are. If I was made to watch this episode in English from end to end without any prior context on said anime, my brain would melt down fairly quickly as well. Just would like to say, though, if they think Asuka's German is bad in the English dub, thank god you didn't show them the Japanese dub.

[]: Post by LeoXiao » Mon May 13, 2013 6:02 pm I don't even think they realized it was German until she started speaking English.

[]: Post by Nouw » Tue May 14, 2013 2:50 am What a bunch of plebs, mocking Evangelion and not being able to appreciate it on their first-viewing. On my first viewing I was in deep philosophical thought over Asuka and Shinji's relationship in this episode and couldn't help but pause and ponder every time something symbolic came up. I just knew after watching the ninth episode this series was a revolutionary classic. . . . Am I doing it right?

[]: Post by Dream » Tue May 14, 2013 2:57 am Nouw wrote: Am I doing it right?

[]: Post by Nouw » Thu May 16, 2013 4:44 am [s]I'm not sure whether to be appalled or amused.[/s] Good work fellas, keeping the filthy casuals out of our fanbase.

[]: Post by Dream » Fri May 17, 2013 4:06 am To be fair, a lot of the critiques in here are regarding the guys's technique rather than their approach. I myself agree that while the commentary wasn't bad it wasn't exactly a comedy riot either.
